key_insights.insight: It's crucial to adapt your management style to the current era, or risk being left behind.
key_insights.quote: So as a coach and as a manager and as a man, I think you need to find a way of how you're moving on with that or you'd be left behind.

key_insights.insight: While praise is essential, it should be delivered thoughtfully and honestly to avoid making individuals 'soft'.
key_insights.quote: I think there's a level of praise you can give people but I think you've also got to be really tough with your praise as well.

key_insights.insight: The process of hiring should involve thorough scouting that extends into character assessment.
key_insights.quote: We'd eventually probably start trying to find out people who know the boy or has played with the boy and try and get a bit of his character background.

key_insights.insight: Trusting your process and experience is key to making sound decisions, even if they don't always work out.
key_insights.quote: But I think really trusting your process and hoping that the longevity I've had will probably hope that you've made more right decisions than wrong decisions.

key_insights.insight: Longevity in a role indicates a sustained level of competence and achievement.
key_insights.quote: Longevity is a really important thing in any work if you can stay in it and you can it's no it's a big thing it's shown that you've done a good enough job

key_insights.insight: Building a team requires establishing a strong foundational culture before prioritizing individual stars.
key_insights.quote: I think while you're trying to build, I think you have to start with a really solid base, good foundation. And then from that point, you try and grow.

key_insights.insight: A supportive partner can provide invaluable strength and perspective during challenging times.
key_insights.quote: My wife's just always stood by me and really supported me whenever comes to the games.

key_insights.insight: Over time, you need to be able to look at the reality of situations and break up a team and change it.
key_insights.quote: I felt as if I nearly had to break it up a little bit because I had seen signs now my experience my longevity was telling me if I don't do this now then I'm going to feel I'm going to be caught out

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize effort and love of the game over pure talent when scouting.
actionable_advice.how_to: Look for individuals who demonstrate dedication, a strong work ethic, and a genuine passion for the field.
Sometimes something just makes you go 'that's not quite what I wanted to hear', and that might only be a gut.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on building a solid foundation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Start with a solid base and good foundation, then you try and grow from that point.

memorable_stories: Sir Alex Ferguson asking David Moyes to be his replacement as manager of Manchester United.
The anecdote about banning chips on a Friday after seeing an overweight player with a side plate of chips.
David's recollection of his mom telling him 'get up and get on with it'.
contrarian_claims: Hiring the most talented or expensive players isn't the guaranteed path to success.
It becomes harder to recruit top talent the more experience you have.
Scottish managers aren't as tough as in the past.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - David Moyes Reveals The Truth About Managing The Worlds Greatest Teams
